symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden unilateral hearing loss after water exposure
symptoms.onset: immediately after water entered the ear while swimming

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sudden occlusion of the canal by hygroscopic wax expansion produces conductive loss.
Sudden unilateral loss mandates excluding a sensorineural cause; cannot be done on history alone.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe deep aural pressure and otalgia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A swollen wax plug pressing on the drum causes deep pressure and pain.
Aural pain is non-specific; also seen in middle-ear infection.
Low-pitched tinnitus commonly accompanies conductive blockage.
Water is absorbed by hygroscopic wax, expanding a pre-existing impaction to full occlusion.
Canal moisture is the classic precipitant of otitis externa.
Cotton buds compact wax deep against the drum, predisposing to impaction.

symptoms.symptom_name: No pain on traction of the pinna/tragus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pain on pinna/tragus movement is characteristic of otitis externa; its absence argues against it.
Fever and discharge would be expected with significant middle/outer ear infection; their absence lowers that probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Otoscopy: dark swollen cerumen plug occluding the tympanic membrane; canal not erythematous; contralateral ear normal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of the wax plug is the diagnostic gold standard — a clinician-only, sealed finding not available via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (afebrile, normotensive, normal heart rate and oxygen saturation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamic stability and being afebrile confirm the absence of systemic illness — a sealed clinician measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Post-removal intact pearly-grey tympanic membrane with restored hearing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immediate resolution after mechanical removal, with an intact drum, confirms benign impaction and excludes perforation and sensorineural loss retrospectively — an in-person outcome.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single required step: visualise the canal and drum to confirm wax, exclude infection/perforation, and gauge whether removal is safe.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Tuning-fork tests / audiometry if hearing does not recover after wax removal
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To exclude sudden sensorineural hearing loss if conductive removal does not restore hearing — time-critical for steroid treatment.
No systemic features; blood tests and imaging are not indicated for benign cerumen impaction.
